stories.title: Knicks Parade Thursday — Canyon of Heroes, 10 AM, Battery Park to City Hall
stories.angle: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has confirmed the full details for the Knicks' championship ticker-tape parade: Thursday June 18, starting at 10:00 AM at Battery Park, traveling north along Broadway through the Canyon of Heroes, ending at City Hall Plaza where a Key to the City ceremony will follow. First Knicks championship parade since 1973 — first in franchise history for many fans. City Hall and municipal buildings will glow blue and orange that day.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
The Knicks parade is OFFICIAL. 🗽

📅 Thursday, June 18
⏰ 10:00 AM
📍 Battery Park → Broadway → City Hall (Canyon of Heroes)

First Knicks championship parade in 53 years. Plan accordingly, NYC. #NewYorkKnicks

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Canyon of Heroes. Battery Park to City Hall. 10 AM Thursday.

After 53 years, the #NewYorkKnicks finally get their ticker-tape parade. Mayor Mamdani presents a Key to the City at City Hall Plaza.

NYC goes BLUE AND ORANGE. 🔵🟠

article.headline: Knicks Parade Thursday: Canyon of Heroes Route, Time, and Everything You Need to Know

research.facts:
Knicks Parade Thursday — Canyon of Heroes
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Parade date: Thursday, June 18, 2026
- Start time: 10:00 AM ET
- Route: Battery Park north along Broadway through Canyon of Heroes to City Hall Plaza
- Post-parade: Key to the City ceremony on City Hall Plaza hosted by Mayor Zohran Mamdani
- City Hall and municipal buildings will be illuminated in blue and orange
- First Knicks championship parade since 1973 (53-year drought)
research.sources:
- Sources: NYC Mayor's Office press release, NBC New York, ABC7 New York, Fox5NY
**Supporting context:**
- Knicks defeated Spurs 94-90 in Game 5 on June 13 at Frost Bank Center
- All-night celebrations in NYC following the win; NYPD reported 63 arrests (Gothamist)
- This is only the third championship in franchise history

stories.title: Brunson's $113M Paycut Built a Championship — The Story Behind the Sacrifice
stories.angle: Two years ago, Jalen Brunson left $113 million on the table by signing a team-friendly 4-year, $156.5M extension instead of waiting for a projected $269M max. That sacrifice created the roster flexibility that let the Knicks build around him. On Saturday night, it paid off with a Finals MVP trophy, a championship ring, and 45 points in the closeout. His father Rick Brunson, a Knicks assistant coach, was on the floor for the celebration — a father-son moment for the ages.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
In 2024, Brunson left $113M on the table to sign a team-friendly deal.

On Saturday, his dad — a Knicks assistant coach — hugged him on the floor as a champion.

Some things are worth more than money. #NewYorkKnicks

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Brunson could have had $269M. He took $156.5M so the Knicks could build a TEAM.

45 points. Finals MVP. Championship ring.

The $113M paycut became the best investment in NBA history. #NewYorkKnicks

article.headline: Brunson Left $113 Million on the Table. Saturday Night, He Got His Return.

research.facts:
Brunson Paycut / Finals MVP Story
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Brunson signed 4-year, $156.5M extension in 2024 (accepted option for 2028-29 season)
- Had he waited until 2025, would have been eligible for 5-year, $269M max deal
- Left approximately $113 million on the table — confirmed by multiple outlets (NBC Sports, Sunday Guardian Live, Posting and Toasting)
- Brunson's Finals stats: 45 points (14-of-27 FG) in Game 5; series average 32.6 PPG, 4.6 APG, 4.2 RPG
- Brunson was unanimously named 2026 NBA Finals MVP (Bill Russell Trophy) — all 11 media voters
- Father Rick Brunson is a Knicks assistant coach
research.sources: - Source: NBA.com, CBS New York, NBC Sports, SNY, Bleacher Report

stories.title: Giannis Trade — Heat Still Frontrunner, 8 Days to June 23 Deadline
stories.angle: With the Finals in the rearview mirror, the Bucks-Giannis saga moves center stage. The Heat remain the frontrunner per insiders Marc Stein and Shams Charania. Proposed Heat package: Tyler Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jaime Jacquez Jr. + prime draft picks. Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam wants a decision before the June 23 draft. Celtics still engaged; Blazers also circling. Giannis has 2 years left on his deal and is eligible for a 4-year, $275M extension on October 1.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Giannis trade update: Heat still frontrunner. Package: Herro + Kel'el Ware + Jacquez Jr. + prime picks.

Bucks want a deal before June 23 draft. That's 8 days.

Celtics and Blazers also in play. Clock is ticking. ⏰

stories.title: Austin Reaves Free Agency — Nets Expected to Offer Max, Lakers Fight to Keep Him
stories.angle: Austin Reaves is the biggest unrestricted free agent of the 2026 offseason. The Brooklyn Nets are expected to offer a 4-year, $178.5M max contract when free agency opens June 30. The Lakers can offer a 5-year, $239M deal. Reaves averaged 23.3 PPG / 5.5 APG / 4.7 RPG on 49% shooting and has expressed desire to stay in LA. The Atlanta Hawks and Detroit Pistons are also in play. Reaves/Luka Doncic pairing is the core of the Lakers' future — losing Reaves would be a major blow.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Nets expected to offer Austin Reaves 4 years, $178.5M max when free agency opens June 30.

Lakers can offer 5 years and $239M — $60M more.

Reaves averaged 23.3 PPG this season. He wants to stay in LA. But Brooklyn will push.

article.headline: Austin Reaves Free Agency: Nets Expected to Offer $178.5M Max as Lakers Fight to Keep Him

research.facts:
Austin Reaves Free Agency
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Reaves is an unrestricted free agent; free agency opens June 30
- Brooklyn Nets expected to offer 4-year, $178.5M max contract (multiple front-office sources)
- Lakers can offer 5-year, $239M deal ($47.8M/yr) — more money than anyone else can offer
- 2025-26 stats: 23.3 PPG, 4.7 RPG, 5.5 APG, 1.1 SPG, 49.0% FG, 36.0% 3PT
- Age: 28
- Atlanta Hawks and Detroit Pistons also expected to show interest
- Reaves has publicly expressed desire to remain with Lakers
- Reaves-Luka Doncic partnership is Lakers' core building block
research.sources: - Sources: Yahoo Sports, NBC Sports, Heavy.com, LakerNation, Bleacher Report

stories.title: NBA Draft 8 Days Out — Dybantsa's Wizards Roster Can Already Compete
stories.angle: AJ Dybantsa's playoff promise to the Wizards looks credible when you see the roster: Trae Young (acquired from Hawks in January), Anthony Davis (from Mavericks at deadline), Alex Sarr, Bilal Coulibaly, and Kyshawn George. Add the 6-9 BYU phenom averaging 25.5 PPG / 51.0% FG and the Wizards could be one of the most exciting young teams in the East. Draft is June 23-24 at Barclays Center, Brooklyn — 8 days away.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Wizards' potential lineup next year:

PG: Trae Young
SF: AJ Dybantsa (No. 1 pick)
C: Anthony Davis

Dybantsa promised DC playoffs. Looking at this roster… he might not be wrong. #NBADraft

article.headline: AJ Dybantsa's Playoff Promise to the Wizards: The Roster Behind the Bold Claim

research.facts:
NBA Draft — Dybantsa and Wizards Roster
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Draft date: June 23-24, 2026, Barclays Center, Brooklyn
- Wizards hold No. 1 overall pick (17-65 record, 14.0% lottery odds)
- AJ Dybantsa: 6-9, BYU, averaged 25.5 PPG / 51.0% FG; -450 odds to go No. 1
- Dybantsa visit to Wizards: around June 11
- Dybantsa quote: "If I go to DC, I'll make the playoffs — I'm gonna make sure I make it"
- Wizards roster additions: Trae Young (from Hawks, January 2026), Anthony Davis (from Mavericks, trade deadline)
- Other young Wizards: Alex Sarr, Bilal Coulibaly, Kyshawn George
- First Washington No. 1 pick since John Wall in 2010
- Darryn Peterson (Kansas): still in No. 1 conversation
research.sources: - Sources: ClutchPoints, Bleacher Report, NBC Sports, Yahoo Sports
